Check Engine Light severity:Variable causes:O2 sensor MAF sensor catalytic converter EVAP leak
Engine Overheating severity:Critical causes:Low coolant failed thermostat blown head gasket blocked radiator
Hard to Start No Start severity:High causes:Dead battery faulty starter fuel pump failure bad ignition
Rough Idle Engine Misfires severity:High causes:Fouled spark plugs dirty injectors vacuum leak low compression
Brake Grinding Squealing severity:Critical causes:Worn brake pads warped rotors seized caliper low brake fluid
Hydraulic System Leak severity:High causes:Seal failure cracked hose damaged cylinder fitting corrosion
Equipment Power Loss severity:High causes:Clogged air filter fuel restriction turbocharger failure EGR
Generator Won't Start severity:Critical causes:Dead battery fuel starvation governor fault injector failure
Electric Motor Overheating severity:High causes:Overload condition blocked ventilation bearing failure voltage unbalance
Motorcycle Chain Slack Noise severity:High causes:Stretched chain worn sprocket improper tension adjustment
O2 Sensor Replacement difficulty:Easy time:30-60 minutes steps:Locate the Faulty Sensor Disconnect Electrical Connector Remove Old Sensor Clean Threads Install New Sensor Clear Codes and Test

Excavator Track Replacement difficulty:Expert time:8-12 hours steps:Position and Secure Machine Release Track Tension Raise the Track Off the Ground Remove the Master Pin Remove Old Track Inspect Undercarriage Components Install New Track and Master Pin

Generator Control Panel Repair difficulty:Expert time:2-4 hours steps:Shut Down and Lock Out Generator Access Control Panel Test Individual Components Replace Faulty Component Clean Connections Test and Validate
Solar Panel Cleaning difficulty:Easy time:1-3 hours steps:Shut Down the Solar System Rinse Panels with Water Scrub with Soft Brush Rinse and Squeegee Dry and Inspect Restart System and Compare Output

Brake Pad and Rotor Replacement difficulty:Moderate time:1.5-2.5 hours steps:Loosen Lug Nuts and Lift Vehicle Remove Wheel and Caliper Remove Old Rotor Install New Rotor Install New Brake Pads Reinstall Caliper and Wheel Bed In New Brakes
Spark Plug Replacement difficulty:Easy time:30-60 minutes steps:Allow Engine to Cool Remove Ignition Coils Remove Old Spark Plugs Gap New Spark Plugs Install New Spark Plugs Reinstall Coils and Test

Hydraulic Hose Replacement difficulty:Moderate time:1-2 hours steps:Relieve System Hydraulic Pressure Identify and Tag Hose Ends Remove Old Hose Assembly Fabricate or Match Replacement Hose Install New Hose and Fittings Bleed System and Pressure Test

Generator Fuel Filter Replacement difficulty:Easy time:20-30 minutes steps:Shut Down Generator Close Fuel Shutoff Valve Remove Old Fuel Filter Install New Filter with Correct Flow Direction Open Valve and Bleed Air Start Generator and Check for Leaks

Electric Motor Bearing Replacement difficulty:Moderate time:2-3 hours steps:Lock Out Tag Out Motor Remove Motor from Machine Disassemble End Bells Press Out Old Bearings Press In New Bearings Reassemble Motor and Test

Motorcycle Chain and Sprocket Replacement difficulty:Moderate time:1.5-2 hours steps:Lift Rear Wheel Remove Master Clip and Old Chain Remove Front and Rear Sprockets Install New Sprockets Route and Join New Chain Adjust Chain Tension and Alignment Test Ride
